description:
Fix two remaining CLI UX issues found during fresh-user experience testing: (1) the 'other MCP clients' fallback instruction in `opentabs start` and `opentabs config show --show-secret` prints a literal `<secret>` placeholder instead of the actual secret value or a helpful reference, and (2) the `opentabs doctor` command does not check for Windsurf MCP config despite Windsurf being listed as a supported client in the MCP config output.

Known LOW-severity issues not addressed here (cosmetic/minor):
- `opentabs plugin search slack` shows empty description when the npm package has no description set — data issue, not CLI issue.
- `opentabs plugin search` with no query may show 'No plugins found' if no public plugins exist and known-slugs lookup finds nothing — acceptable edge case.

userStories.title: Replace literal <secret> placeholder with actual secret in MCP client fallback text
userStories.description: As a new user configuring a non-listed MCP client, I want the fallback instruction text to include the actual secret value so that I can copy a working Authorization header without having to run a separate command to find my secret.
acceptanceCriteria: The line in printMcpClientConfigs that says 'pointing to ... with Authorization: Bearer <secret>' interpolates the actual secret value when secret is non-null
When secret is null, the text says 'with an Authorization: Bearer header' (no placeholder that looks like it should be replaced)
The fallback line also mentions 'opentabs config show --show-secret' as an alternative for retrieving the secret later

userStories.title: Add Windsurf config path to doctor MCP client config check
userStories.description: As a Windsurf user running opentabs doctor, I want the MCP client config check to detect my Windsurf configuration so that the doctor report accurately reflects my setup instead of showing a false warning.
acceptanceCriteria: defaultMcpClientLocations includes a Windsurf entry with path ~/.codeium/windsurf/mcp_config.json
The Windsurf entry checks for an 'opentabs' key inside 'mcpServers' (matching the config format shown in printMcpClientConfigs)
The doctor hint message for 'no MCP client configured' includes Windsurf as an option alongside Claude Code, Cursor, and OpenCode
userStories.notes:
File: platform/cli/src/commands/doctor.ts, lines ~230-267.

Current `defaultMcpClientLocations` only has Claude Code, Cursor, and OpenCode:
```ts
const defaultMcpClientLocations = (): McpClientLocation[] => [
  { name: 'Claude Code', path: join(homedir(), '.claude', 'settings', 'mcp.json') },
  { name: 'Cursor', path: join(process.cwd(), '.cursor', 'mcp.json') },
  { name: 'OpenCode', path: join(process.cwd(), 'opencode.json') },
];
```

Add Windsurf entry:
```ts
{ name: 'Windsurf', path: join(homedir(), '.codeium', 'windsurf', 'mcp_config.json') },
```

The Windsurf config uses the `mcpServers` key (same format as Cursor), so the existing `hasMcpServersOpentabs` check in `checkMcpClientConfig` (line ~246) already handles it — no logic changes needed there.

Also update the hint message at line ~266 to mention Windsurf:
```
'Add an "opentabs" entry to ~/.claude/settings/mcp.json (Claude Code), .cursor/mcp.json (Cursor), opencode.json (OpenCode), or ~/.codeium/windsurf/mcp_config.json (Windsurf)'
```

Note: `homedir` is already imported at the top of the file (line 20).
